questions.text: Two triangles are on the same base and between the same parallels. What can be said about their areas?

options.c: One area is double the other

questions.explanation: Triangles on the same base and between the same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: A parallelogram and a rectangle are on the same base and between the same parallels. Their areas are:
questions.explanation: Parallelogram and rectangle on the same base and between same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: The area of a triangle is half the area of a parallelogram if both are on the same base and between the same parallels. True or False?
questions.explanation: Area of triangle = 1/2 area of parallelogram on same base and between same parallels.

questions.text: A median of a triangle divides it into two parts having areas in the ratio:
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal area parts.

questions.text: If two quadrilaterals are on the same base and between the same parallels, their areas are:

options.b: Quadrilateral with longer side has more area

questions.explanation: Figures between same parallels and on same base have equal area.

questions.text: The area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 8 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area = 1/2 × base × height = 1/2 × 10 × 8 = 40 cm².

questions.text: If a parallelogram has base 12 cm and height 5 cm, its area is:
questions.explanation: Area = base × height = 12 × 5 = 60 cm².

questions.text: Two triangles have equal bases and equal heights. Their areas are:
questions.explanation: Triangles with equal base and height have equal areas.

questions.text: If a triangle's median is drawn, what fraction of the total area does each smaller triangle have?
questions.explanation: Median divides the triangle into two equal areas, each half of total area.

questions.text: The area of a quadrilateral can be found by dividing it into:
questions.explanation: Area of quadrilateral = sum of areas of two triangles formed by its diagonal.

questions.text: If two parallelograms are on the same base and between the same parallels, then:

options.b: One area is double the other
options.d: One area is half the other

questions.explanation: Parallelograms on same base and between same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: Which of the following figures has an area equal to half the area of a parallelogram on the same base and between the same parallels?
questions.explanation: Triangle area is half the parallelogram area on same base and between same parallels.

questions.text: If the height of a parallelogram is doubled while keeping the base same, its area will:
questions.explanation: Area = base × height, so doubling height doubles area.

questions.text: If a triangle and parallelogram have the same base and height, the ratio of their areas is:
questions.explanation: Triangle area is half the parallelogram area with same base and height.

questions.text: A triangle has vertices A, B, and C. The median from vertex A meets BC at D. The areas of triangles ABD and ADC are:
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal area parts.

questions.text: Two triangles are on the same base but between different parallels. What can be said about their areas?

options.c: One area is double the other

questions.explanation: Triangles between different parallels on same base have different areas.

questions.text: A parallelogram ABCD and rectangle ABFE lie on the same base AB and between the same parallels AB and DF. If area of ABCD is 50 cm², then area of rectangle ABFE is:
questions.explanation: Parallelogram and rectangle on same base and between same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: If the diagonal of a parallelogram divides it into two triangles, the area of each triangle is:

options.c: Equal to the area of parallelogram

questions.explanation: Diagonal divides parallelogram into two equal area triangles.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, diagonal AC divides it into two triangles. If area of triangle ABC is 30 cm² and area of triangle ADC is 20 cm², then area of quadrilateral ABCD is:
questions.explanation: Area of quadrilateral = sum of areas of two triangles formed by diagonal.

questions.text: If two triangles have equal areas and one has base 8 cm and height 5 cm, the height of the other triangle with base 10 cm is:
A parallelogram and a rectangle stand on the same base of length 20 m and between the same parallels. If the height of the parallelogram is 12 m, what is the area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Parallelogram and rectangle on the same base and between the same parallels have equal areas. Area = base × height = 20 × 12 = 240 m².

questions.text: A triangle has a base of 15 cm and a height of 8 cm. A parallelogram on the same base and between the same parallels has what area?
questions.explanation: Area of triangle = 1/2 × base × height = 1/2 × 15 × 8 = 60 cm². Parallelogram on same base and height has double the area = 120 cm².

questions.text: In triangle ABC, median AD divides it into two parts. If the area of triangle ABC is 72 cm², what is the area of triangle ABD?
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal areas, so area of ABD = 72/2 = 36 cm².

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD has diagonals AC = 10 cm and BD = 24 cm intersecting at right angles. Find the area of ABCD.
questions.explanation: Area of quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals = 1/2 × AC × BD = 1/2 × 10 × 24 = 120 cm².

questions.text: Two triangles are on the same base and between the same parallels. If the area of one triangle is 45 cm², what is the area of the other triangle?
questions.explanation: Triangles on same base and between same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: A parallelogram and a triangle stand on the same base of 18 m and between the same parallels. If the height is 10 m, find the ratio of their areas.
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 18 × 10 = 180 m²; Area of triangle = 1/2 × base × height = 90 m²; Ratio = 180:90 = 2:1.

questions.text: The area of a parallelogram is 150 cm² and its base is 15 cm. Find the height.
questions.explanation: Area = base × height; height = Area/base = 150/15 = 10 cm.

questions.text: In a triangle, a median divides it into two parts. If the area of one part is 50 cm², what is the area of the whole triangle?
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal areas, so total area = 2 × 50 = 100 cm².

questions.text: A rectangle and a parallelogram are on the same base and between the same parallels. If the rectangle has length 25 m and height 8 m, what is the area of the parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Both have equal area = base × height = 25 × 8 = 200 m².

questions.text: The diagonals of a rhombus are 16 cm and 30 cm. Find its area.
questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = 1/2 × product of diagonals = 1/2 × 16 × 30 = 240 cm².

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D is the midpoint of BC. A line through D is drawn parallel to AB intersecting AC at E. If the area of triangle ABC is 72 cm², what is the area of quadrilateral ABED?
questions.explanation: Since D is midpoint of BC and DE is drawn parallel to AB, quadrilateral ABED lies between the same parallels AB and DE, and triangle ADE is half of triangle ABC. Area of triangle ADE = 36 cm², so area of quadrilateral ABED = Area of triangle ABC - Area of triangle ADE = 72 - 36 = 36 cm².

questions.text: Parallelogram ABCD and rectangle ABEF are on the same base AB and between the same parallels AB and CD. If the area of parallelogram ABCD is 120 cm² and length AB = 15 cm, find the area of rectangle ABEF given its length AB = 15 cm and width EF = 8 cm. Are the areas equal? Justify.

options.a: Area of rectangle = 120 cm²; Areas are equal because they lie between same parallels on same base.
options.b: Area of rectangle = 120 cm²; Areas are not equal because width differs.
options.c: Area of rectangle = 15 × 8 = 120 cm²; Areas are equal as both lie between same parallels on same base.
options.d: Area of rectangle = 15 × 8 = 120 cm²; Areas are not equal because rectangle height differs from parallelogram height.

questions.explanation: Area of rectangle = length × width = 15 × 8 = 120 cm². Since parallelogram and rectangle lie on same base AB and between same parallels AB and CD, their areas are equal. Hence, both areas are 120 cm².

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M is the midpoint of side QR. A line through M is drawn parallel to PQ intersecting PR at N. If area of triangle PQR is 90 cm², find the area of quadrilateral PMNR.
questions.explanation: Since M is midpoint of QR and MN is drawn parallel to PQ, quadrilateral PMNR lies between the same parallels PQ and MN. Triangle PMN is half the area of triangle PQR (since MN is parallel and M is midpoint). Therefore, area of triangle PMN = 45 cm². Quadrilateral PMNR = triangle PQR - triangle PMN = 90 - 45 = 45 cm².

questions.text: Diagonal AC of quadrilateral ABCD divides it into two triangles ABC and ADC. If the median of triangle ABC divides it into two equal areas of 30 cm² each, and the area of triangle ADC is 50 cm², find the area of quadrilateral ABCD.
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle ABC into two equal areas each of 30 cm², so area of triangle ABC = 60 cm². Area of triangle ADC = 50 cm². Therefore, area of quadrilateral ABCD = 60 + 50 = 110 cm².

questions.text: A parallelogram and a triangle are on the same base and between the same parallels. The parallelogram's area is 150 cm². A median of the triangle divides it into two parts of equal area. If one part has area 40 cm², find the area of the triangle and compare it with the parallelogram's area.

options.a: Triangle area = 80 cm²; parallelogram area is greater.
options.b: Triangle area = 80 cm²; parallelogram and triangle areas are equal.
options.c: Triangle area = 40 cm²; parallelogram area is greater.
options.d: Triangle area = 80 cm²; triangle area is greater.

questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal areas, so total area of triangle = 2 × 40 = 80 cm². Parallelogram area is 150 cm², which is greater than triangle's 80 cm², even though both are on same base and between same parallels (parallelogram area = base × height, triangle area = ½ × base × height).

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, diagonal AC divides it into two triangles ABC and ADC. If the area of triangle ABC is 70 cm² and a median of triangle ADC divides it into two equal parts, find the area of the smaller part formed by the median in triangle ADC and the total area of parallelogram ABCD.

options.a: Smaller part = 35 cm²; Parallelogram area = 140 cm²
options.b: Smaller part = 70 cm²; Parallelogram area = 210 cm²
options.c: Smaller part = 35 cm²; Parallelogram area = 70 cm²
options.d: Smaller part = 70 cm²; Parallelogram area = 140 cm²

questions.explanation: Diagonal divides parallelogram into two equal triangles, so area of triangle ADC = 70 cm². Median divides triangle ADC into two equal parts, so smaller part = 70 ÷ 2 = 35 cm². Total area of parallelogram = 70 + 70 = 140 cm².

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D is the midpoint of BC. If the area of triangle ABC is 72 cm², find the area of triangle ABD. Justify your answer using the property of median dividing a triangle into two equal areas.
questions.explanation: The median divides the triangle into two equal areas, so area of ABD = ½ × area of ABC = 36 cm².

questions.text: Parallelogram PQRS and rectangle PXYZ are on the same base PQ and between the same parallels PQ and RS. If the area of parallelogram PQRS is 84 cm², find the area of rectangle PXYZ.
questions.explanation: Parallelogram and rectangle on the same base and between the same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, the median AM is drawn from vertex A to side BC. If the area of triangle ABC is 90 cm², find the area of triangle AMC.
questions.explanation: Median divides the triangle into two equal areas, so area of AMC = ½ × 90 = 45 cm².

questions.text: Two triangles ABC and DEF are on the same base BC and between the same parallels BC and AD. If the area of triangle ABC is 60 cm², what is the area of triangle DEF? Explain.
questions.explanation: Triangles on the same base and between the same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD has diagonal AC dividing it into triangles ABC and ADC. If the area of triangle ABC is 40 cm² and the area of triangle ADC is 35 cm², find the area of quadrilateral ABCD.
questions.explanation: Area of quadrilateral = sum of areas of two triangles = 40 + 35 = 75 cm².

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, a diagonal AC divides it into two triangles ABC and ADC. If the area of triangle ABC is 50 cm², find the area of triangle ADC.
questions.explanation: Diagonal of parallelogram divides it into two triangles of equal area.

questions.text: Triangle ABC and parallelogram ABDE are on the same base AB and between the same parallels AB and DE. If the area of triangle ABC is 30 cm², find the area of parallelogram ABDE.
questions.explanation: Area of triangle is half the area of parallelogram on same base and between same parallels.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, median PM is drawn to side QR. If the area of triangle PMR is 25 cm², find the area of triangle PQR.
questions.explanation: Median divides triangle into two equal areas, so total area = 2 × 25 = 50 cm².

questions.text: Two parallelograms ABCD and EFGH are on the same base AB and between the same parallels AB and DC. If the area of parallelogram ABCD is 120 cm², find the area of parallelogram EFGH.
questions.explanation: Parallelograms on the same base and between the same parallels have equal areas.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, diagonal BD divides it into triangles ABD and BCD. If the area of triangle ABD is 55 cm² and the area of triangle BCD is 45 cm², find the total area of quadrilateral ABCD.
questions.explanation: Area of quadrilateral = sum of areas of two triangles = 55 + 45 = 100 cm².

questions.case_text: A farmer in Punjab has two rectangular fields, Field A and Field B, both having the same base length of 50 metres. Field A is a rectangle with a height of 30 metres, while Field B is a parallelogram with the same base and height. The farmer wants to compare the areas of these fields to decide which one to cultivate this season.

subparts.text: What is the area of Field A?
subparts.explanation: Area of rectangle = base × height = 50 × 30 = 1500 m².

subparts.text: What is the area of Field B?
subparts.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 50 × 30 = 1500 m², same as Field A.

subparts.text: If the farmer divides Field B into two triangles by drawing a diagonal, what is the area of each triangle?
subparts.explanation: Area of each triangle = half of parallelogram = 1500 ÷ 2 = 750 m².

questions.case_text: In a village near Jaipur, a school plans to build a playground in the shape of a quadrilateral ABCD. They know that the diagonal AC divides the playground into two triangles ABC and ADC. The lengths of the sides are AB = 40 m, BC = 30 m, CD = 50 m, DA = 30 m, and the diagonal AC = 60 m. The school wants to find the total area of the playground by calculating the areas of the two triangles.

subparts.text: If triangles ABC and ADC lie between the same parallels AB and DC, what can be said about their areas?

options.c: Area of ABC is double ADC

subparts.explanation: Triangles between same base but not necessarily same height; areas generally unequal unless proven.

subparts.text: If the height corresponding to base AC in triangle ABC is 24 m, what is the area of triangle ABC?
subparts.explanation: Area = ½ × base × height = ½ × 60 × 24 = 720 m².

subparts.text: If the height corresponding to base AC in triangle ADC is 18 m, find the total area of quadrilateral ABCD.
subparts.explanation: Area of ADC = ½ × 60 × 18 = 540 m²; total area = 720 + 540 = 1260 m².
